hypothesis: Training CHROMA with a mixed objective that combines token-level cross-entropy (weight 0.5) and a differentiable TMM-based CIE76 ∆E sequence loss optimized via a straight-through Gumbel-Softmax relaxation over the discrete material–thickness tokens will reduce the mean held-out validation error from ~34 to ≤20 ∆E without increasing TP error above 2 ∆E for the N=8 decoder under the same dataset and constraints.
